text.title: Agentic RAG Tool-Using — Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every tool-using RAG agent must obey: bounded loops with logs, specific tool descriptions, cheap routing model, intra-run caching, allow-list for external search.

rule.statement: Cap the tool-using loop at max_calls (recommended 3). Each iteration MUST log {iteration, tool_name, input_query, result_summary_truncated_to_200_chars, latency_ms} to a structured sink.

rationale.source: LangGraph agentic RAG tutorial + production failure post-mortems

rule.rationale: Uncapped loops let confused selectors loop indefinitely on ambiguous queries. Without logs, tool-selection becomes a black box that cannot be debugged or audited in production.

rule.statement: Each tool description passed to the LLM MUST distinguish purpose, input shape, and result shape — e.g. "vector_search(query:str) returns top-k document chunks by semantic similarity" vs "keyword_search(query:str) returns exact phrase matches" — not just tool names.
rule.rationale: Ambiguous descriptions cause the LLM to default to the first tool regardless of query type, defeating the registry. Specific descriptions let the model route on intent, not order.

rule.statement: Routing decisions use a fast/cheap model (haiku, gpt-4o-mini). The final synthesis step uses a heavier model (opus, gpt-4o). Routing and synthesis token budgets are tracked separately.
rule.rationale: Tool routing is classification, not reasoning — Opus burns ~10x the budget per call for no quality gain on the decision itself. Synthesis benefits from the heavier model.

rule.statement: Cache tool results keyed by (tool_name, normalised_query) within a single agentic run. Cache MUST be cleared between runs to avoid stale answers.

rationale.source: Observed loop pathologies in iterative retrieval

rule.rationale: The same sub-query can recur across iterations; re-executing wastes latency and API cost without adding new information. Cross-run caching introduces freshness bugs.

rule.statement: If web_search is in the registry, ship an explicit domain allow-list. Calls to non-listed domains MUST be rejected by the wrapper before the network call.
rule.rationale: Unrestricted web search can retrieve adversarial pages, out-of-date data, or content that conflicts with the vetted corpus. Allow-list is the only configuration-time defence.
